diagrams.title: PPP Frame Format
diagrams.description: Complete structure of PPP frame showing all fields in sequence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding PPP encapsulation and frame boundaries

diagrams.title: PPP Three Phase Connection Establishment
diagrams.description: Shows the three phases of PPP connection: Link Establishment, Authentication, Network Layer Protocol
diagrams.purpose: Understanding PPP connection lifecycle and state transitions

diagrams.title: Character Escaping in PPP
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how special characters are escaped when appearing in information field
elements: Original flag (0x7E) becomes escape (0x7D) followed by (0x7E XOR 0x20 = 0x5E)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding byte stuffing mechanism for async PPP

diagrams.title: PAP vs CHAP Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of PAP and CHAP authentication flows
elements: PAP: Client sends credentials → Server validates → Server responds (2 steps)
CHAP: Server sends Challenge → Client sends Response (MD5 hash) → Server validates (3 steps)
Show security difference: PAP transmits plaintext, CHAP transmits hashed value
diagrams.purpose: Understanding authentication protocol differences and security implications

visuals.title: Point To Point Protocol
visuals.description: Visual diagram for point to point protocol
